title: The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition 2026: Global Writing Contest for Young Writers Across the Commonwealth
summary: The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition (QCWC) 2026 is now officially open for submissions, inviting young writers from across the Commonwealth and beyond to share their creativity, ideas, and voices with the world. Organized by the Royal Commonwealth Society (RCS) , the Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition is the world s oldest international writing competition for schools , with a remarkable history dating back to 1883 . For more than a century, this prestigious global competition has provided a platform for young people to express their ideas, explore global themes, and develop their creative writing skills. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Each year, thousands of young writers from across the Commonwealth participate in the competition, submitting stories, essays, and other written works that reflect their perspectives on global issues and shared experiences. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); For the 2026 edition , participants are invited to respond to the inspiring theme: Common Ground: Better Together. Young writers are encouraged to reflect on how shared values, cooperation, and collaboration can bring communities together and help address global challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The competition deadline is 30 April 2026. About The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition is one of the flagship youth initiatives of the Royal Commonwealth Society , an organization dedicated to promoting education, equality, sustainability, and global cooperation across Commonwealth nations. Since its inception in the late 19th century, the competition has served as a powerful platform for young voices. It encourages participants to engage with global themes and express their ideas through creative writing. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Over the years, the competition has helped nurture generations of talented writers, many of whom have gone on to achieve remarkable accomplishments in literature, journalism, public service, and academia. The competition has grown significantly in recent years. In 2025 alone, the competition received over 53,000 entries from around the world , demonstrating its global impact and the enthusiasm of young writers eager to share their perspectives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Through this competition, the Royal Commonwealth Society seeks to empower youth by giving them the opportunity to explore important issues affecting their communities and the wider world. 2026 Theme: Common Ground Better Together The theme for the 2026 Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition is Common Ground: Better Together. The Commonwealth is one of the most diverse international communities in the world, spanning multiple continents and representing a wide array of cultures, languages, landscapes, and generations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Its member countries range from small island nations to vast and populous states. While these countries differ in geography, history, and cultural traditions, they are connected by shared values and common aspirations for a better future. The theme encourages participants to explore questions such as: What unites communities despite their differences? How can cooperation and partnership drive positive change? What shared challenges do Commonwealth nations face today? How can young people contribute to building stronger and more inclusive communities? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Participants are encouraged to reflect creatively on how collaboration, mutual understanding, and shared responsibility can help address the challenges facing societies today. Through storytelling, essays, poetry, or other creative writing forms, entrants can explore these ideas and share their unique perspectives. Why This Competition Matters The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition plays a crucial role in encouraging young people to engage with global issues through creative expression. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Writing allows participants to: Develop their creativity and communication skills Reflect on social and global issues Express their ideas and perspectives Build confidence as young writers Connect with peers across different cultures and countries The competition also promotes literacy, critical thinking, and storytelling as tools for social awareness and personal development. By participating in this global contest, young writers join a vibrant international community that celebrates creativity, diversity, and shared human values. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); A Legacy of Distinguished Alumni Over the years, many participants and winners of the Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition have gone on to achieve remarkable success in literature and beyond. The competition has served as an early platform for talented young writers whose creativity and insight have shaped their future careers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); These alumni demonstrate how powerful storytelling and thoughtful writing can influence society, inspire change, and open doors to new opportunities. The Royal Commonwealth Society celebrates the achievements of past participants who have used their voices to contribute to meaningful conversations about global issues. The Evolution of the Competition The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition continues to evolve to reflect the changing landscape of global storytelling and youth engagement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Formerly known as the Queen s Commonwealth Essay Competition , the initiative has been reimagined to embrace a broader definition of writing and creativity. This new identity reflects a more inclusive approach to storytelling, welcoming diverse forms of written expression such as: Essays Short stories Poetry Creative narratives Reflective writing This transformation ensures that the competition remains accessible, innovative, and relevant to young writers from all backgrounds. How to Participate Young writers who wish to participate in the competition must submit their entries through the official competition platform provided by the Royal Commonwealth Society. Participants should carefully review the following before submitting their entries: Official entry form Competition guidelines Submission requirements Terms and conditions Frequently asked questions Resources are also available to help participants prepare their entries and understand the competition requirements. All submissions must reflect the 2026 theme Common Ground: Better Together. Global Reach of the Commonwealth Community The Commonwealth represents one of the most diverse global communities, connecting countries across Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and the Pacific. Despite differences in culture, geography, and history, Commonwealth nations share common values rooted in cooperation, mutual respect, and a commitment to improving lives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition celebrates this diversity while encouraging young people to reflect on the shared values that unite communities. By bringing together voices from across the Commonwealth, the competition highlights the importance of dialogue, understanding, and collaboration in shaping a better future. Application Deadline All entries for The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition 2026 must be submitted by: 30 April 2026 Participants are encouraged to submit their entries before the deadline to ensure they meet all competition requirements. Click HERE to learn more and apply. For more global competitions, fellowships, grants, scholarships, conferences, and many others, subscribe to OFY . title: African Commonwealth Students’ Convention 2026 in Uganda: Apply Now to Join Africa’s Next Generation of Student Leaders
summary: Applications are now open for the African Commonwealth Students Convention 2026 , a major gathering designed to bring together student leaders, youth innovators, policymakers, and institutional actors from across the African Commonwealth. The convention will take place in Uganda from April 12 to April 16, 2026 , offering a unique platform for young changemakers to collaborate on solutions that advance Africa s transformation through education, innovation, science, and technology. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Held under the framework of the Commonwealth Students Association (CSA) , this convention provides an opportunity for students and youth leaders to share ideas, build networks, and contribute to discussions that influence the continent s development agenda. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the African Commonwealth Students Convention The African Commonwealth Students Convention is designed to empower young people to play a central role in Africa s development. It brings together students, youth leaders, innovators, and policymakers to exchange ideas and develop practical solutions to pressing challenges facing the continent. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants will engage in discussions, workshops, and collaborative sessions that focus on strengthening leadership, innovation, and policy engagement among African youth. The convention also provides a valuable platform for delegates to showcase their ideas, initiatives, and projects aimed at creating positive change in their communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); What the Convention Covers Selected participants will receive support for all on-ground arrangements during the event. The convention will cover: Accommodation throughout the convention Meals during the program Access to all sessions, workshops, and convention activities Networking opportunities with leaders, innovators, and policymakers ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Participants will only be required to cover their travel or flight costs to Uganda . Key Thematic Areas Applicants can select the thematic area that best aligns with their interests and experience. The convention will focus on the following themes: Education Reform Innovation and Entrepreneurship Science and Technology Governance and Policy Rural Youth Empowerment Regional Integration Other related areas (applicants may specify) These themes are designed to encourage innovative thinking and collaborative problem-solving to address key challenges across Africa. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Benefits of Participating The African Commonwealth Students Convention offers several benefits for participants, including: Opportunities to network with student leaders and policymakers from across Africa A platform to share ideas and innovative solutions Exposure to discussions on education reform, technology, and governance Leadership development and collaboration opportunities Participation in workshops and policy-focused dialogues Application Timeline Applications are currently open for the African Commonwealth Students Convention 2026. While a specific application deadline has not been announced, applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to the competitive nature of the program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The convention will take place from April 12 to April 16, 2026, in Uganda , and early application allows selected participants enough time to make travel arrangements. How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official application through the convention s application platform. Applicants are typically required to provide information about their leadership experience, interests, and preferred thematic area. APPLY HERE. Early applications are strongly encouraged. Discover more conference opportunities, click here . title: Promise Venture Studio Opens $200K Build Award and $20K Imagine Award for Early Childhood Innovators
summary: Early childhood innovators and community leaders now have a powerful opportunity to secure funding and support to scale their impact. Promise Venture Studio has launched two funding tracks through the Promise Fund designed to support founders working to improve outcomes for children ages 0 5 and the families and communities that support them. The two awards the Build Award and the Imagine Award provide flexible funding, mentorship, and community support for early-stage innovators developing solutions that transform early childhood systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); These opportunities aim to close a critical funding gap in early childhood innovation while empowering leaders who are deeply connected to the communities they serve. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Why Early Childhood Innovation Matters Despite the enormous impact that early childhood investments can have on lifelong outcomes, the sector remains chronically underfunded. The Promise Fund was created to address this gap by investing in early-stage innovators and organizations focused on building equitable, community-rooted solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Promise Venture Studio believes that leaders with strong community ties often hold the most powerful insights for transforming systems that serve young children and their families. By providing early capital and capacity-building support, the Promise Fund helps innovators test ideas, refine models, and grow sustainable organizations. The program focuses specifically on solutions that benefit children ages 0 5, as well as parents, caregivers, and communities that nurture early development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The Build Award: $200,000 for Early-Stage Early Childhood Organizations The Build Award is designed for high-potential early-stage organizations that are ready to grow their impact. Selected organizations will receive $200,000 in unrestricted funding along with a 12-month partnership with Promise Venture Studio. The award supports organizations led by community-connected founders who are working to create equitable outcomes for children and families. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Participants gain not only funding but also access to a comprehensive development program designed to strengthen organizational capacity and long-term sustainability. What Build Award Recipients Receive Successful applicants will benefit from a year-long program that combines funding, mentorship, and professional development support. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Key benefits include: $200,000 in flexible funding to advance organizational goals A 12-month strategic partnership with Promise Venture Studio Direct one-on-one support from the Promise team Personalized mentoring and expert guidance Capacity-building support focused on growth and impact In-person convenings for learning, collaboration, and reflection Access to an ongoing alumni network and peer community The program aims to help early-stage organizations strengthen their impact models, build sustainable strategies, and attract additional funding opportunities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Build Award Deadline The preliminary application window runs from March 2, 2026, to April 10, 2026 . APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Organizations selected from the preliminary round will be invited to submit a full application due on July 2, 2026 . Applicants invited to complete the full application will receive an $800 honorarium for their time and effort. Finalists will also have access to optional impact workshops designed to help them communicate their impact model and strengthen their funding readiness. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); The Imagine Award: $20,000 for Idea-Stage Early Childhood Innovators The Imagine Award supports early childhood changemakers who are at the idea stage and ready to test transformative concepts through real-world pilots. The award provides $20,000 in flexible funding along with a six-month learning experience designed to help innovators refine their ideas and test them with real users. Participants will develop practical skills in piloting solutions, collecting feedback, and refining their approaches to maximize impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); What Imagine Award Participants Receive Imagine Award recipients receive a combination of funding and tailored support to help them turn ideas into actionable pilots. Program benefits include: $20,000 in flexible grant funding Six-month cohort-based learning program One-on-one partnership and coaching from the Promise team Mentorship from experienced leaders and experts Cohort workshops focused on pilot design and implementation A multi-day in-person convening for collaboration and learning Continued alumni support and peer networking ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Participants will also learn how to build effective feedback loops, test early solutions with users, and communicate their vision to attract future partnerships and funding. Imagine Award Deadline Applications for the Imagine Award will open during the week of July 6, 2026 , and will close on August 28, 2026 . NOTIFY WHEN APPLICATIONS ARE OPEN HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Finalists will be notified during the week of September 28, 2026 . Applicants can also participate in free pilot workshops during July and August to design a low-cost or no-cost pilot plan that can be implemented within three to six months. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Investment Criteria The Promise Fund evaluates applicants based on several key criteria that ensure projects align with its mission of advancing early childhood equity. Focus on Early Childhood Funded ideas and ventures must focus on children ages 0 5 and the parents, caregivers, and communities that support them. Community-Connected Leadership Priority is given to leaders who have direct connections to the communities they serve and whose lived experience informs their solutions. Equity-Driven Solutions Selected ventures must demonstrate a commitment to equitable outcomes for young children and their caregivers. Long-Term Sustainability Applicants should demonstrate a clear vision for sustainable impact and organizational growth. Community-Centered Design Solutions should be rooted in direct input from families and communities, ensuring programs meet real needs and priorities. Why This Opportunity Stands Out The Promise Fund represents a new model for equitable early-stage investment. By combining flexible funding with mentorship, peer learning, and capacity-building support, the program helps innovators move from ideas to scalable solutions. For founders working to transform early childhood systems, these awards provide a rare opportunity to secure capital, mentorship, and a supportive network designed to accelerate impact. Early childhood innovators with bold ideas and strong community ties are encouraged to apply and take advantage of this unique funding opportunity. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Discover more grant opportunities, click here . The post Promise Venture Studio Opens $200K Build Award and $20K Imagine Award for Early Childhood Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Young Women’s Advisory Group Supporting Girls’ Access to Sport in Europe
summary: Young women across Europe are invited to apply for the Young Women s Advisory Group , a new initiative under the Playing for Purpose program by the adidas Foundation . The program aims to amplify the voices and experiences of young women to help shape initiatives that make sport and physical activity more inclusive and accessible. This opportunity is open to women aged 18 29 living in Germany , France , and Spain , especially those with multicultural or migration backgrounds and an interest in sport, youth empowerment, and community engagement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Young Women s Advisory Group Sport should be a welcoming space for everyone. However, many girls and young women particularly those from multicultural backgrounds living in Europe still face barriers to participation. To address this, the adidas Foundation is launching the Young Women s Advisory Group to ensure that young women s voices, ideas, and lived experiences inform the development of programs designed to support girls and young women in sport. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Members of the advisory group will serve in a consultative role , providing insights, recommendations, and feedback that help guide the foundation s upcoming project aimed at improving access to sports and encouraging girls to stay active. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); While the foundation will make the final decisions, the perspectives and input from advisory group members will play an important role in shaping project design, implementation, and evaluation. What Participation Involves The advisory group is designed to be flexible and manageable alongside studies, work, and personal commitments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Participants can expect: Online meetings and discussions In-person workshops , including one event in Germany Around two hours of commitment per month Activities scheduled in advance with clearly defined expectations Advisory group members will receive a fixed allowance in recognition of their time, expertise, and lived experiences. To ensure participation is accessible, the adidas Foundation will also cover travel, accommodation, and related costs for in-person meetings. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Who Should Apply This opportunity may be a great fit if you: Live in ** Germany , France , or Spain Identify as a woman aged 18 29 Have experience with or an interest in sport Work with, support, or care about young people Bring perspectives shaped by multicultural or migration backgrounds Applicants who are passionate about making sport more inclusive and empowering for young women are strongly encouraged to apply. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Application Process Interested candidates should first review the full Call for Applications to learn more about the role, expectations, and selection process. How to Apply Read the full Call for Applications Complete the online application form The application will require basic information along with a few short responses about your motivation, experience, and interest in joining the advisory group. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Shortlisted applicants may be invited for a brief online conversation . All applicants will be notified once the selection process is complete. Deadline 22 March 2026 at 23:59 CET Click here to discover more opportunities. The post Apply Now: Young Women’s Advisory Group Supporting Girls’ Access to Sport in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme 2026: Fully Funded Cloud Computing Training for Ghanaian STEM Graduates (Free Certification, Stipends, and Job Pathways)
summary: The demand for cloud computing professionals is growing rapidly across the world. Organizations in every industry from finance and healthcare to e-commerce and telecommunications are moving their infrastructure to the cloud. To address this demand and empower young professionals with future-ready skills, AmaliTech collaborates with Amazon Web Services to deliver the AWS re/Start Programme, a career-focused training initiative designed to prepare individuals for entry-level roles in cloud computing. Introduction The AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme is a fully funded cloud computing training initiative designed to equip unemployed or underemployed graduates with in-demand digital skills for careers in cloud technology. The programme is implemented by AmaliTech in partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS) through the global AWS re/Start Programme initiative. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This highly competitive technology training programme provides participants with the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in cloud computing, prepare for globally recognized AWS certifications, and develop professional skills needed to secure employment in the rapidly growing tech industry. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The programme is specifically targeted at Ghanaian graduates who are passionate about launching a career in cloud computing but may not yet have the industry experience required to enter the technology workforce. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); By offering structured learning, career coaching, certification preparation, and job-readiness training, the programme serves as a bridge between academic knowledge and real-world employment opportunities in the digital economy. Overview of the AWS re/Start Programme The AWS re/Start Programme is a global workforce development initiative designed to prepare individuals for entry-level cloud computing careers. It focuses on providing learners with foundational IT knowledge, practical cloud skills, and career support that can help them secure jobs in technology and IT support roles. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); In collaboration with AmaliTech, the programme has been adapted to empower young African professionals with the skills needed to compete in the global technology workforce. Participants undergo intensive training that covers key aspects of cloud computing, networking, programming, databases, and cybersecurity. By the end of the programme, learners are equipped with the technical and professional competencies required to pursue roles such as: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Cloud support associate Technical support specialist Systems administrator IT support engineer Cloud operations associate These roles are among the fastest-growing career paths in the global technology sector. Programme Duration and Structure The AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme is structured as a 12-week intensive training programme delivered entirely online. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Participants are required to commit to a full-time learning schedule, dedicating approximately 8 hours per day from Monday to Friday to the programme. The programme is delivered in cohorts, meaning applicants are grouped into batches that begin training at specific times during the year. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 2026 Cohorts January April April July July October This cohort-based structure ensures that participants benefit from collaborative learning, mentorship, and peer networking throughout the programme. Benefits of the AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme Participants selected for the programme receive a wide range of benefits that go beyond technical training. 1. Free Cloud Computing Training The programme is completely free, allowing participants to access high-quality cloud computing education without financial barriers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); 2. AWS Certification Exam Voucher Participants receive a voucher to take an AWS certification exam, enabling them to obtain a globally recognized credential that enhances their employability. 3. Stipends and Internet Support To ensure learners can fully participate in the programme, selected candidates receive stipends and internet bundles to support their studies. 4. Industry-Relevant Curriculum The curriculum is developed and delivered by experts and aligns with current industry standards and employer expectations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); 5. Professional Network Development Participants gain access to a growing community of instructors, mentors, and technology professionals who can provide career guidance and opportunities. 6. Soft Skills and Career Preparation In addition to technical training, learners develop critical professional skills such as: Communication and teamwork Resume and CV development Interview preparation Career planning These skills significantly improve participants chances of securing employment after completing the programme. Skills and Knowledge You Will Learn The programme provides comprehensive training in several key areas of cloud computing and IT infrastructure. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Cloud Computing Fundamentals Participants begin by learning the fundamentals of cloud computing and how modern organizations use cloud platforms to manage applications and data. Linux and System Administration Learners gain practical experience with Linux operating systems , which are widely used in cloud environments. Networking The programme covers essential networking concepts such as: IP addressing Network architecture Routing and switching Network security Python Programming Participants learn the basics of Python programming, enabling them to automate tasks and manage cloud infrastructure. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Database Fundamentals Training includes an introduction to database systems and how they are deployed in cloud environments. AWS Core Services Students gain practical knowledge of key AWS services used by organizations worldwide. This combination of skills prepares participants for real-world IT and cloud support roles. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme, applicants must meet specific eligibility requirements. Nationality Requirement Applicants must be Ghanaian citizens living in Ghana. Educational Qualification Candidates must hold a tertiary degree in a STEM-related field, such as: Computer Science Information Technology Engineering Mathematics or related disciplines Employment Status Applicants must currently be: Unemployed, or Underemployed and seeking a career in cloud computing. Technical Requirements Applicants must have: Access to a computer or laptop with webcam and microphone A reliable internet connection for online training sessions. Commitment Requirement Candidates must be able to participate in full-time training sessions for 8 hours daily from Monday to Friday during the programme duration. Passion for Cloud Computing Applicants should demonstrate strong motivation to pursue a career in cloud technology. Career Opportunities After Completing the Programme Graduates of the AWS re/Start Programme are well-positioned to pursue entry-level roles in the cloud computing and IT sectors. Possible career paths include: Cloud Support Associate IT Support Engineer Systems Administrator DevOps Support Engineer Technical Operations Specialist Because cloud computing continues to grow globally, professionals with AWS skills are increasingly in demand across industries. Why the AWS re/Start Programme Is a Valuable Opportunity The global demand for cloud computing professionals has increased dramatically as organizations migrate their operations to cloud platforms. By combining: industry-recognized certification preparation, hands-on technical training, professional development support, and connections to potential employers, ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Application and Selection Process The selection process for the programme is designed to identify motivated candidates with the potential to succeed in cloud computing careers. Step 1: Online Application Interested candidates must complete the official application form through the programme s website. Apply here Step 2: Application Screening Submitted applications are reviewed to ensure candidates meet the eligibility criteria. Step 3: Phone Interview Shortlisted candidates participate in a phone interview to assess motivation and suitability for the programme. Step 4: Learner Assessment Applicants complete an assessment sent via email to evaluate their aptitude for the programme. Step 5: Admission Notification Successful candidates receive an official email confirming their admission into the programme. Application Deadline 31st March 2026 The AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme provides a comprehensive pathway for young graduates to transition into high-demand technology careers.For many participants, the programme serves as the first step toward a long-term career in cloud computing and digital infrastructure. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme 2026: Fully Funded Cloud Computing Training for Ghanaian STEM Graduates (Free Certification, Stipends, and Job Pathways) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Schwarzman Academy Fellowship 2026–2027 at Chatham House: Fully Funded 10-Month Global Policy Leadership Fellowship in London
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious Schwarzman Academy Fellowship 2026 2027 hosted by Chatham House, the Royal Institute of International Affairs in London. This highly competitive fellowship offers emerging global leaders the opportunity to conduct policy-relevant research, engage with global experts, and develop leadership skills through an intensive ten-month fellowship programme. The fellowship is offered through The Queen Elizabeth II Academy for Leadership and the Next Generation at Chatham House and is designed to support early- to mid-career professionals who are committed to shaping evidence-based solutions to global challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The programme will begin in October 2026 and will run for ten months. During this period, fellows will work closely with Chatham House researchers, participate in policy dialogues and events, and contribute to research outputs that address pressing international issues. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The application deadline for the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship 2026 2027 is 20 March 2026 at 5 pm GMT. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); About Chatham House Chatham House, officially known as the Royal Institute of International Affairs, is one of the world s leading independent policy institutes. Based in London, the institution has built a global reputation for conducting high-quality research, facilitating dialogue on international affairs, and promoting evidence-based policy solutions. The organization s mission is to help build a sustainably secure, prosperous, and just world. Through research programmes, policy dialogues, and international partnerships, Chatham House addresses major global challenges including geopolitical conflict, economic transformation, environmental sustainability, global health, and international governance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The Queen Elizabeth II Academy for Leadership and the Next Generation was established to cultivate the next generation of global policy leaders. The Academy provides structured fellowship programmes designed to equip participants with leadership capabilities, analytical skills, and networks needed to address complex international challenges. Overview of the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship The Schwarzman Academy Fellowship is a ten-month in-person fellowship that provides an exceptional platform for Schwarzman Scholars completing their master s degree to further develop their expertise in global policy research and leadership. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Fellows will join a cohort of international professionals drawn from academia, civil society, government, business, and media sectors. Participants will contribute to Chatham House research programmes while also developing their leadership capabilities through a structured Leadership Masterclass Programme. The fellowship combines independent research with collaborative engagement across Chatham House s global network of experts, policymakers, and practitioners. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Fellowship Structure and Responsibilities During the ten-month fellowship, Schwarzman Academy Fellows will engage in several core activities that support both their professional development and the research mission of Chatham House. Fellows will conduct independent research on global policy themes or geographic regions aligned with the research priorities of Chatham House programmes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Participants will contribute to Chatham House research initiatives, publications, and events, offering their expertise and insights into key global policy debates. Fellows will also collaborate with a broad network of scholars, policymakers, journalists, and civil society leaders connected to Chatham House. In addition, participants will take part in the Leadership Masterclass Programme, which is designed to strengthen policy leadership skills and prepare fellows for influential roles in global affairs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Leadership Masterclass Programme The Leadership Masterclass Programme is an integral part of the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship. It provides fellows with structured opportunities to develop leadership capabilities and strategic thinking skills relevant to international policy environments. Through seminars, workshops, and mentoring sessions, fellows will build skills in leadership, policy communication, media engagement, and strategic analysis. Participants will also receive writing support, career mentoring, and coaching to help them translate research insights into impactful policy contributions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The programme aims to cultivate a new generation of global leaders capable of shaping policy solutions in increasingly complex geopolitical environments. Research Focus Areas Applicants must propose research that aligns with the priorities of one of the Chatham House research programmes. These programmes focus on major global policy issues and regional developments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Research themes include topics related to Africa s digital transformation, governance and digital public infrastructure, the geopolitics of digital connectivity, and digital trade negotiations. Other areas include the global energy transition and strategies for achieving sustainable resource use. Applicants may also focus on European strategic sovereignty, industrial policy integration, and Europe s engagement with rising global powers. In the area of global economic policy, research may explore the future of the US dollar in the international monetary system or the impact of artificial intelligence on the global economy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Global health research topics include African health sovereignty, geopolitical dynamics in health diplomacy, and strengthening global health security systems. Additional areas include international law, security studies, conflict resolution, Latin American geopolitics, Middle East governance and regional dynamics, Russian political economy, Central Asian transformation, UK foreign policy strategy, and US global economic and security policies. Applicants are encouraged to develop innovative research proposals that align with Chatham House s mission and contribute to evidence-based policy solutions. Benefits of the Fellowship Schwarzman Academy Fellows receive a comprehensive benefits package designed to support their participation in the fellowship and their professional development. Fellows receive a monthly stipend of 2,565 to cover living expenses in London, including accommodation, utilities, food, and transport. Chatham House also covers relocation costs including travel, visa fees, and accommodation support. Successful applicants requiring a visa will be sponsored under the UK Temporary Work Government Authorised Exchange visa scheme. The fellowship also covers the UK Immigration Health Surcharge, though dental, optical care, and prescription costs are not included. Fellows are provided with research funding to support fieldwork, outreach activities, policy events, and publications. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Participants also gain full membership to Chatham House during the fellowship, providing access to exclusive events, research resources, and expert networks. After completing the programme, fellows receive a five-year Chatham House membership. Professional Development and Networking The fellowship provides extensive networking opportunities with global policymakers, academics, and practitioners. Participants engage with Chatham House staff, Academy faculty, and a global alumni network that includes leaders across government, international organizations, and policy institutions. These networks often open pathways to careers in international organizations, government advisory roles, think tanks, and global policy institutions. Location and Programme Format The Schwarzman Academy Fellowship is based in London and operates on a hybrid working model. Fellows are expected to spend a minimum of two days per week in the Chatham House office, though some weeks may require additional in-person participation for leadership programme activities and events. The fellowship is a full-time commitment and participants cannot undertake other work or academic study during the programme. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); Application Requirements Applicants must meet specific academic and professional criteria to be eligible for the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship. Applicants must be current Schwarzman Scholars who are due to complete their Schwarzman Scholars master s degree in 2026. Candidates must have experience conducting independent research and must hold at least a bachelor s degree or have equivalent professional experience. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(140); }); Applicants should demonstrate leadership potential, a strong commitment to international policy work, and alignment with the mission and values of Chatham House. Proficiency in English is required. Applicants must also submit a research proposal that aligns with one of the Chatham House research programmes and demonstrates originality and policy relevance. If you require assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at academy@chathamhouse.org. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(141); }); Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship 2026 2027 is 20 March 2026 at 5 pm GMT. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and early submission is strongly encouraged. Applicants will be notified of the outcome within four weeks after the application deadline. How to Apply Interested applicants must complete the official application form and submit their CV along with a research proposal and supporting application materials. Learn more and apply HERE . For questions regarding the fellowship, applicants may contact the Academy team via email at academy@chathamhouse.org . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(142); }); Conclusion The Schwarzman Academy Fellowship at Chatham House represents an exceptional opportunity for emerging global policy leaders to deepen their expertise, conduct impactful research, and strengthen their leadership capabilities. Through research collaboration, leadership development, and engagement with one of the world s most respected international affairs institutions, fellows gain the skills and networks necessary to influence global policy debates and contribute to solutions for the world s most pressing challenges. Click HERE to discover more Fellowship opportunities on OFY. title: Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Residency Program 2027 in Italy (Fully Funded): A Prestigious 26-Day Global Residency for Scholars, Artists, and Changemakers
summary: The Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Residency Program 2027 is now open for applications, offering an extraordinary opportunity for global leaders, scholars, artists, and practitioners to advance transformative projects that address the world s most pressing challenges. Hosted at the historic Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center in Lake Como, Italy , the residency provides a 26-day immersive experience where selected participants receive time, resources, and a collaborative environment to focus deeply on their work. For nearly 70 years , the Bellagio Center has supported visionary thinkers and innovators whose ideas and projects have contributed to meaningful global impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Applications are open for the 2027 cohort , and interested applicants must submit their expression of interest by March 20, 2026 (5 PM EDT) . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); This prestigious residency has welcomed many of the world s most influential thinkers and leaders, including over 85 Nobel Laureates and renowned figures such as economist Amartya Sen, economist Milton Friedman, author Maya Angelou, former President of Ireland Mary Robinson, and U.S. Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The program continues to serve as a powerful platform where individuals from diverse disciplines collaborate, exchange ideas, and develop breakthrough solutions that benefit humanity. About the Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Located in the scenic region of Lake Como, Italy , the Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center is one of the world s most renowned convening spaces for global leaders and innovators. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The center provides a serene and intellectually stimulating environment designed to foster deep thinking, interdisciplinary dialogue, and collaboration among experts from around the world. For decades, Bellagio has supported the development of groundbreaking ideas, influential publications, policies, artistic works, and global initiatives that have shaped discussions around development, equity, and human well-being. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Through the Bellagio Center Residency Program, the Rockefeller Foundation aims to: Advance innovative solutions to global challenges Encourage collaboration across disciplines and sectors Support leaders working to improve human well-being Strengthen global partnerships that drive positive change Participants join a dynamic community of thinkers and creators dedicated to building a more equitable and sustainable world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Learn more about the Bellagio Center HERE . Program Overview: What the Bellagio Residency Offers The Bellagio Center Residency Program offers selected participants a unique opportunity to focus intensely on their projects while engaging with a diverse group of global leaders. During the 26-day residency , fellows will work alongside a cohort of approximately 14 scholars, artists, and practitioners from different disciplines and regions of the world . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Residents benefit from uninterrupted time for creative and intellectual work while also participating in cross-disciplinary discussions that spark new ideas and collaborations. The program includes the following benefits: Fully Supported Residency Experience Participants receive a fully supported stay at the Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center, including: A 26-day residency in Lake Como, Italy Private studio space for focused work Accommodation and meals (room and board) Travel funding to and from Italy Access to a vibrant community of international leaders and experts Interdisciplinary Collaboration Residents become part of a diverse, cross-sector cohort where individuals from different professional backgrounds exchange ideas, offer feedback, and collaborate on innovative approaches to global challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); This interdisciplinary environment often leads to new partnerships, projects, and long-lasting professional relationships. Lifelong Global Network Participants also become members of the Bellagio Network , an international community of alumni who remain connected through collaborations, initiatives, and partnerships long after their residency ends. Who the Bellagio Center Residency Program Is Looking For The Rockefeller Foundation seeks exceptional leaders and innovators whose work has already demonstrated impact and who are developing bold ideas capable of improving the well-being of humanity. Successful applicants typically demonstrate: Significant achievement and recognition in their field A strong track record of advancing impactful ideas or initiatives Commitment to improving global well-being Openness to collaboration and interdisciplinary exchange Willingness to actively participate in the Bellagio alumni network ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The program welcomes leaders from diverse disciplines including academia, arts, policy, science, journalism, nonprofit leadership, and the private sector. Types of Projects Supported The Bellagio Center Residency Program supports projects that generate meaningful social impact and contribute to solving major global challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Applicants are encouraged to propose projects that: Offer innovative solutions to global problems Generate new knowledge or insights Inspire action or policy change Benefit vulnerable or underserved populations Advance public good rather than private profit Projects may include research publications, books, policy frameworks, artistic productions, organizational strategies, or other initiatives aimed at creating meaningful change. Key Thematic Areas While the Bellagio Residency accepts applications from any discipline through its general open call, the Rockefeller Foundation also prioritizes projects aligned with several thematic areas addressing critical global issues. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Universal Energy Abundance Projects that expand access to clean, affordable, and reliable energy for communities worldwide, particularly those historically underserved by current energy systems. Food Systems and Nutrition Innovative approaches to building sustainable, nutritious, and resilient food systems that benefit consumers, producers, and the environment. Economic Mobility Ideas and initiatives that help individuals access stable employment, better wages, and long-term financial security , while addressing systemic barriers to opportunity. Health Projects addressing emerging global health challenges, including climate-driven health threats such as extreme heat , and strengthening resilient health systems. Artificial Intelligence for Good Innovations that harness AI responsibly to address global challenges such as education, healthcare, and development while promoting transparency and accountability. Catalytic Finance Financial innovations designed to mobilize capital for underserved communities and climate-vulnerable regions , including blended finance and outcome-based funding models. Climate and Development Strategies that integrate climate resilience into development efforts , helping communities adapt while maintaining sustainable economic growth. Eligible Applicants and Project Categories The Bellagio Center Residency Program welcomes a wide range of leaders and professionals, including: Academics and Scholars Researchers, professors, and scientists from universities or think tanks whose work advances knowledge and scholarship in their fields. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); These projects often result in books, academic articles, or influential research outputs . Artists Creative professionals such as: Writers and authors Poets and playwrights Filmmakers and visual artists Musicians and composers Dancers and performers Artistic projects should demonstrate how they contribute to social impact and ensure public access to the final work . Practitioners Senior professionals including: Nonprofit leaders Journalists Policy advocates Private-sector innovators Public leaders These projects may produce policy briefs, strategic plans, research publications, or advocacy initiatives that support vulnerable populations and address global challenges. The Bellagio Residency Experience The Bellagio Center residency is designed to foster creativity, reflection, and collaboration. Residents enjoy uninterrupted time to focus on their projects while also participating in discussions and exchanges with other fellows. This environment encourages: Cross-disciplinary dialogue Collaborative problem-solving New intellectual partnerships Innovative thinking Many groundbreaking ideas and influential publications have emerged from conversations and collaborations formed during Bellagio residencies. Global Impact and Legacy Each year, the Bellagio Center welcomes approximately 100 residents from around the world . These individuals represent a diverse range of disciplines, experiences, and perspectives, creating a vibrant intellectual community focused on solving global challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); Over the decades, Bellagio residents have contributed to major breakthroughs in areas such as: Economic policy Social justice Scientific innovation Public health Climate action Arts and culture Through its residency program, the Rockefeller Foundation continues to support visionary leaders who are shaping the future of humanity. Application Deadline Applications for the Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Residency Program 2027 must be submitted by: March 20, 2026 (5 PM EDT) Applicants must submit their expression of interest through the official Rockefeller Foundation application portal . All applications and participation are subject to the Bellagio Center Residency Program Terms and Conditions . Click HERE to discover more global Fellowship opportunities. The post Rockefeller Foundation Bellagio Center Residency Program 2027 in Italy (Fully Funded): A Prestigious 26-Day Global Residency for Scholars, Artists, and Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Marine Conservation Finance Research Call – WIOMSA MARG 2026
summary: Are you an early-career researcher or graduate student working on marine science, conservation, or ocean finance? The Western Indian Ocean Marine Science Association (WIOMSA), in partnership with the Blue Nature Alliance (BNA), has issued a thematic research call under its Marine Research Grant (MARG) Programme to strengthen how marine conservation finance is understood and practiced across the Western Indian Ocean (WIO) region . This call focused on marine conservation finance invites applied, field-based research proposals that explore ways to make long-term financing systems more sustainable, resilient, and equitable. With marine conservation ambitions expanding across the region, including commitments to Global Biodiversity Framework targets and 30 30 objectives, there is a pressing need for robust evidence and innovative financial strategies to support protected areas, coastal communities, and blue economy initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); What This Call Is About Traditional funding models for marine conservation often rely on short-term or project-based contributions that leave critical programs exposed to economic and geopolitical risks. Fragmented revenue, fluctuating budgets, and narrow funding streams can undermine long-term planning for Marine Protected Areas (MPAs), Locally Managed Marine Areas (LMMAs), and other essential conservation frameworks. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); To help address these challenges, WIOMSA and the Blue Nature Alliance are seeking research that builds a strong evidence base on financing systems for marine conservation in the Western Indian Ocean. The aim is to generate practical insights that can inform national, regional, and policy frameworks for durable and diversified conservation finance . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Applicants are encouraged to explore topics including, but not limited to: Quantifying financing needs and identifying gaps in current funding frameworks Assessing potential revenue diversification strategies and financial resilience Evaluating governance and institutional structures that support effective finance systems Linking financial stability to measurable conservation outcomes and performance metrics Analysing the implications of economic shocks on marine conservation financing Why This Research Matters Marine conservation in the Western Indian Ocean a region that includes countries such as Kenya, Tanzania, Mozambique, Seychelles, and Madagascar is integral to both ecological resilience and the wellbeing of coastal communities. Healthy marine ecosystems underpin fisheries, tourism, coastal protection, and cultural heritage. However, even well-designed conservation programs can falter without reliable, coordinated financial systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Thematic research supported under this call will help bridge the gap between conservation planning and financial sustainability. By producing evidence on financing gaps, governance structures, and revenue diversification strategies, researchers contribute to a more predictable, equitable, and impact-oriented conservation finance landscape in the WIO. This work can influence policy decisions, investment priorities, and long-term sustainability strategies for marine ecosystems across the region. For early career and postgraduate researchers, this call offers not only funding but also the chance to be part of a growing community focused on ocean sustainability and finance innovation a field increasingly recognised as critical to achieving global biodiversity and climate goals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Eligibility and Who Should Apply This call is specifically targeted at: Early Career Ocean Professionals (ECOPs) Ongoing Master s and PhD students currently enrolled at recognised institutions within the WIO region Individuals whose research aligns with strengthening marine conservation finance dynamics in the region Proposals should be field-based and designed to generate data and insights that inform actionable strategies for improving financing systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Grant Support and Structure Successful proposals will receive support for field-based research activities, with grants covering expenses directly related to: Data collection and fieldwork Research materials and logistics Stakeholder engagement efforts tied to the project ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Selected research proposals will receive financial support to carry out field-based research related to marine conservation finance, including costs for fieldwork, stakeholder engagement, and data collection Timeline How to Apply Deadline for proposals: 30 March 2026 Applicants should complete the online submission form provided by WIOMSA. Learn more and apply on the official page . Interested researchers are encouraged to read the full call for proposals carefully to understand priority themes, eligibility criteria, and submission requirements. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Looking for more opportunities? Explore the latest global calls on the Opportunities for Youth website. The post Apply Now: Marine Conservation Finance Research Call – WIOMSA MARG 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for DASIE 2026 – Fully Funded Data Analytics Immersion at Carnegie Mellon University
summary: Are you an undergraduate student curious about how data shapes scientific discovery and research careers? The Data Analytics for Science Immersion Experience (DASIE) at Carnegie Mellon University (CMU) offers a fully funded, immersive summer experience designed to introduce students to the real-world use of data analytics across scientific disciplines. The program brings together students from accredited U.S. colleges and universities including those on student visas to explore the intersection of data and science while building community, learning from experts, and contemplating future academic and professional paths. This nine-day residential experience takes place at CMU s Pittsburgh campus in May 2026, and is tailored for current undergraduate students and first-year master s students interested in data-informed scientific work. All travel, lodging, transportation, and meals are covered, and participants receive a stipend, making DASIE not just an educational opportunity but an accessible one. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); What DASIE Is All About DASIE is a weeklong immersion experience hosted by the Mellon College of Science (MCS) at Carnegie Mellon University. It is designed to introduce students to the role of data analytics in science by connecting them with faculty, researchers, and practitioners who use data in their work. The program recognizes the growing importance of data skills across scientific fields and aims to help students understand how data informs questions, decisions, and innovation in scientific research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); During DASIE, students take part in structured tutorial sessions, workshops, presentations, and discussions led by CMU faculty and experts from organizations such as the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center . These sessions showcase examples of data-driven scientific work and highlight career and academic paths that data analytics can unlock. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); In addition to scheduled program activities, DASIE provides space for participants to network with peers from a range of institutions, meet faculty and staff, and build community. Group activities encourage reflection on students own interests and goals while exposing them to diverse uses of data across scientific contexts. Why DASIE Matters As data becomes central to nearly every scientific discipline from genomics to climate modeling, from drug discovery to astrophysics students with data literacy and analytical skills gain a competitive edge in both academic and professional arenas. Programs like DASIE help bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world scientific data practices at world-class research institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Beyond the technical exposure, DASIE offers students the chance to meet faculty, industry professionals, and fellow students with shared interests. These interactions help participants expand their understanding of how data shapes decision-making and scientific inquiry, and can inspire the pursuit of graduate work or careers in data-rich environments. Students who have participated in past iterations of DASIE report gaining clarity about their academic goals, building confidence in their capabilities, and connecting with communities and pathways they might not have otherwise explored. Some have even gone on to launch student organizations or pursue advanced studies in data science and analytics. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); What Participants Experience The DASIE program runs from May 12 to May 20, 2026 , with on-campus programming from May 13 19. Session days typically begin early and include a mix of instructional content, hands-on activities, and professional development. A group activity off campus fosters community and adds a fun learning dimension outside the classroom. All participants receive the following as part of the fully funded experience: Round-trip airfare or mileage reimbursement to Pittsburgh Stipend for incidental expenses Hotel accommodations in double rooms nearby campus All meals throughout the program period Daily transportation between campus, hotel, and activity sites ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); While DASIE is not a for-credit course or a research position, it offers an enrichment experience focused on exposure to scientific uses of data, opportunities for mentoring, and guidance for thinking about next steps whether academic, research-focused, or professional. Who Should Apply DASIE is open to current undergraduate students and first-year master s students from accredited U.S. institutions. There is no minimum GPA requirement , and the program does not restrict citizenship students on valid study visas may apply, though they must maintain their eligibility and documentation independently. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The experience is especially relevant for students in STEM majors, including biology, chemistry, mathematics, computer science, engineering, and related fields, who want to learn how data analytics informs scientific work and research. Beyond academic background, strong applicants will be curious about data in science, eager to engage with mentors and peers, and ready to explore how data skills can enhance their future career or educational trajectory. Application Timeline How to Apply Interested candidates should prepare their applications carefully, as DASIE is competitive and designed for motivated students who want to deepen their understanding of data in science. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Final deadline: March 13, 2026 Program dates: May 12 20, 2026 Send completed applications with required materials (including recommendation letters for shortlisted applicants) to the program as indicated on the official page. Looking for more opportunities? Explore the latest global calls on the Opportunities for Youth website. The post Apply for DASIE 2026 – Fully Funded Data Analytics Immersion at Carnegie Mellon University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Nigeria Climate Youth Negotiators Programme 2026: Apply to Represent Nigeria in Global Climate Negotiations
summary: Young Nigerian professionals working in climate governance now have a unique opportunity to gain global negotiation experience through the Nigeria Climate Youth Negotiators Programme (CYNP) 2026. Organized by the National Council on Climate Change (NCCC), the programme is designed to equip emerging climate leaders with the skills and knowledge required to actively participate in international climate negotiations. Through structured mentorship, professional training, and exposure to global policy platforms, the CYNP seeks to strengthen Nigeria s voice in climate diplomacy while preparing the next generation of negotiators. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Climate Youth Negotiators Programme (CYNP) The Climate Youth Negotiators Programme is an initiative aimed at developing the technical and diplomatic capacities of young public officials engaged in climate-related work across Nigerian government institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Participants will undergo intensive training designed to prepare them for international climate discussions, particularly those under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC). The programme combines academic learning, policy analysis, and real-world negotiation simulations to ensure participants are well prepared for the complexities of climate diplomacy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); By the end of the programme, participants will be better equipped to represent Nigeria in global climate dialogues and contribute to the country s climate governance framework. Programme Benefits Participants selected for the CYNP 2026 cohort will gain access to several valuable opportunities that will enhance their professional development and influence in climate policy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Key benefits include: Six months of intensive training on climate negotiations and UNFCCC processes Mentorship from experienced climate negotiators and policymakers Practical training in negotiation strategy and international diplomacy Opportunities to draft policy briefs and position papers Exposure to national and global climate governance structures Networking opportunities with experts in climate policy and sustainability Support for participation at COP 31 Increased professional credibility within Nigeria s climate leadership community What Participants Will Learn The programme provides a comprehensive learning experience designed to strengthen participants understanding of international climate policy and negotiations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Participants will develop skills in: Climate policy analysis Negotiation strategy and diplomatic engagement International climate governance processes Technical dialogue participation Drafting negotiation texts and policy positions They will also gain insights into the operational processes of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change and other global environmental agreements. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Who Should Apply The programme is specifically designed for early-career government professionals who are already involved in climate-related work within Nigeria s public sector. Applicants must be actively contributing to climate policy, environmental management, sustainability programmes, or related initiatives within government institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The programme aims to strengthen the capacity of public officials who can represent Nigeria effectively in global climate policy discussions. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Climate Youth Negotiators Programme 2026 is 10 March 2026 . APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more short courses opportunities, click here . Interested and eligible candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the closing date. Why This Programme Matters Climate negotiations are shaping the future of global environmental policy, and developing countries need strong representation in these discussions. By investing in the next generation of negotiators, the Climate Youth Negotiators Programme helps ensure that Nigeria s perspectives and priorities are effectively represented in international climate forums. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); For young professionals committed to climate governance and global environmental diplomacy, this programme provides a pathway to become influential voices in shaping climate policy.
